seoTitle: How to Change an IP Address in Cloudflare (2026)
metaDescription: Update the IP address in your Cloudflare A or AAAA records when migrating servers, and confirm the change with a live ping test.
intro: Moving to a new server means your Cloudflare DNS records need to point at the new IP, or visitors will keep hitting your old host. This guide walks through editing your A and AAAA records safely during a migration.

steps.heading: Open the DNS Records grid
steps.body: Select your domain and navigate to the DNS Records panel in the sidebar.

steps.heading: Find your root A record
steps.body: Locate your main website A record and click Edit.

steps.heading: Replace the old IP
steps.body: Clear out the old server IP and paste in your new hosting destination address.

steps.heading: Check subdomain pointers too
steps.body: Review related subdomain records like www or mail to make sure they align with the new target where needed.

steps.heading: Save and test
steps.body: Adjust your proxy status and TTL as needed, save the change, and run a live ping test to confirm the new routing is working.

faq.question: Do I need to update AAAA records too, or just A records?
faq.answer: If your new server supports IPv6, you'll want to update the AAAA record as well; otherwise you can remove it if it's no longer applicable.

faq.question: Will changing my IP address cause downtime?
faq.answer: There can be a brief propagation window while the change spreads across DNS resolvers, so testing at a low-traffic time helps minimize disruption.

faq.question: Should I proxy the record after changing the IP?
faq.answer: Keeping it proxied (orange cloud) preserves Cloudflare's caching and security benefits, while DNS Only exposes your new server's IP directly.

faq.question: How do I confirm the new IP is actually live?
faq.answer: Run a ping or dig command from your terminal after saving to confirm the domain now resolves to the updated address.
